medications.drug_name: IM ketorolac / opioids / cannabinoids over chat
medications.indication_rationale: Injections and strong/controlled agents cannot be safely initiated via telehealth and could mask a septic joint; not the safe first step.
An oral NSAID (within limits, if no contraindication) is reasonable for arboviral arthralgia, but standard doses have already failed; a GP can review a regular course after assessment.
medications.dose_route_frequency: within recommended limits; GP to review

medications.indication_rationale: Regular paracetamol within limits as baseline analgesia while assessment is arranged.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Urgent fall-safety assessment — declutter, raised toilet seat/bed aids, transfer aids — for a non-weight-bearing isolated patient.
Non-weight-bearing joint mobility and graded reconditioning as the flare settles.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Gentle non-weight-bearing joint movement, joint rest, warmth
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Comfort and stiffness reduction while awaiting assessment.
No acute evidence base for arboviral arthralgia; not recommended (cannabinoids also not appropriate to initiate remotely).

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for any single joint becoming fiery red/hot/hugely swollen or a high fever with chills, and seek urgent care if they occur.
Avoid risky transfers, clear tripping hazards, and arrange help/aids before mobilising; use a raised toilet seat if available.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces fall risk in a cluttered home while alone.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use regular paracetamol within limits; do not add more OTC anti-inflammatories beyond doses without advice.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like a flare of your post-Ross River joint pain — painful and disabling, but not the joint-destroying kind of arthritis.

Because your joints can't be examined over a chat, we do need an in-person joint check, and we watch carefully for a joint infection — a single hot, red, hugely swollen joint or a high fever means urgent care.
Injections and strong painkillers aren't the safe answer remotely; keep to regular paracetamol within limits while we arrange help.
Because you can't weight-bear and you're on your own in a cluttered home, let's sort out fall-safety — clear hazards, get help to move, and use aids — so you don't have a fall.

follow_up_plan.interval: In-person GP review within 1–2 days for a joint examination and fall-safety; sooner if worsening
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Joint examination, inflammatory markers/serology, analgesia plan, fall-safety and mobility
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: A single fiery joint, high fever, or a fall — seek urgent/emergency care.

must_include_items: Safety-net septic arthritis (single hot/red/swollen joint, high fever)
Arrange in-person joint assessment and urgent fall-safety support
acceptable_alternatives: An oral NSAID within limits if no contraindication
Same-day assessment if the patient cannot manage safely at home
errors_of_commission: Attributing severe joint pain to a flare with no safety-netting
Reflexive ambulance for a stereotyped non-septic flare
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) septic-arthritis safety-netting, (2) no remote strong/controlled analgesia, and (3) an in-person joint assessment plus fall-safety support. Prescribing injections/opioids on demand or ignoring the fall risk is a fail.
